title: Fixing IndexError list index out of range in Synthesizer.generate_goldens_from_docs()
body:
This fixes https://github.com/confident-ai/deepeval/issues/1682

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Fixes an IndexError in Synthesizer.generate_goldens_from_docs() by enforcing aligned (contexts, source_files, scores) outputs from ContextGenerator and by guarding/capping multiple indexing sites in Synthesizer’s sync/async contexts→goldens pipeline (including an async variable-shadowing bug that mis-indexes source_files).

steps.operation: Normalize and validate ContextGenerator outputs before invoking the contexts→goldens pipeline (ensure contexts/source_files/scores are parallel, equal-length lists; truncate or filter defensively if needed, and fail fast with a clear error if invariants cannot be restored).
steps.reason: Even if ContextGenerator is fixed, the docs entry point is where the mismatched lists first cross the boundary into Synthesizer; adding an invariant check here prevents downstream IndexError and avoids silently associating wrong metadata to contexts.
steps.side_effects: May change behavior from crashing with IndexError to raising a clearer ValueError/log warning; downstream code will see consistent list lengths; may require updating/adding tests that assert the invariant.

steps.operation: Apply the same invariant enforcement/normalization for the async docs entry point (mirror sync behavior, including any truncation/filtering strategy and error messaging).
steps.reason: The async path must be as safe as the sync path; otherwise issue #1682 can still reproduce when users call the async API.
steps.side_effects: Async callers may observe clearer errors instead of IndexError; potential slight overhead for validation.

steps.operation: Fix out-of-range indexing when evolving inputs per context: cap iteration to max_goldens_per_context (or size progress-id lists based on actual count) and guard indexing of pbar_evolve_input_ids; also rely only on aligned (contexts, source_files, context_scores) when indexing by context i.
steps.reason: Current code sizes progress-bar IDs using max_goldens_per_context but enumerates qualified_synthetic_inputs which can exceed that cap, causing IndexError; additionally, this function assumes perfect alignment of ContextGenerator outputs and should be robust given the bug history.
steps.side_effects: May reduce number of evolved inputs processed to the configured cap (more predictable output); progress reporting may change; tests expecting more-than-cap evolution may need updates.

steps.operation: Mirror the sync fixes in the async orchestration: ensure per-context tasks do not assume misaligned metadata, and ensure any progress-id or per-input iteration is capped/guarded consistently with the sync implementation.
steps.reason: Async orchestration passes indices/metadata into workers; any mismatch or uncapped indexing can still trigger IndexError in async workflows.
steps.side_effects: Async output counts may become capped more strictly; concurrency behavior unchanged but per-task bookkeeping may differ; tests for async generation may need adjustment.

steps.operation: Fix variable shadowing and wrong indexing in the async per-context worker: rename inner per-input index variable, use the context_index for source_files/context_scores association, and guard/cap pbar_evolve_input_ids indexing to the number of tracked items (or build IDs sized to actual processed inputs).
steps.reason: The current implementation shadows the context index with a per-input index, leading to source_files[index] using the wrong index and causing IndexError/corrupted metadata; it also repeats the progress-id indexing hazard.
steps.side_effects: Fixes incorrect source_file attribution in generated goldens for async runs; may affect logging/progress semantics; requires regression coverage to ensure no IndexError and correct metadata.

steps.operation: Ensure generate_contexts returns strictly parallel lists: when building per-file contexts, only append a score when a context is actually appended; similarly append matching source_file entries; optionally add an internal assertion that lengths match before returning.
steps.reason: Issue stems from misalignment created during context sampling/scoring; enforcing the invariant at the generator boundary prevents downstream indexing assumptions from breaking and avoids mismatched context-score associations.
steps.side_effects: May slightly change which scores are returned (dropping scores for skipped/empty contexts); downstream ranking/selection could shift but will be correct and stable; may require test updates.

steps.operation: Apply the same parallel-list invariant enforcement in the async generator entry point, ensuring the returned contexts/source_files/scores lengths always match and represent the same items in the same order.
steps.reason: Async and sync generators must produce equivalent, aligned outputs; otherwise async callers can still experience IndexError or incorrect context-score pairing.
steps.side_effects: Potential minor differences in async output ordering/selection if previously relying on buggy misalignment; may require updating async-specific tests.

steps.operation: Fix the primary mismatch source in per-source-file context building: move scoring append to occur only after context validity checks pass (non-empty chunk, sufficient similar chunks), and ensure any early-continue branches do not append to scores/source_files without also appending the corresponding context.
steps.reason: This function currently can score sampled chunks and then skip creating/appending a context, producing len(scores) > len(contexts); fixing this eliminates the root cause of downstream IndexErrors and incorrect associations.
steps.side_effects: May reduce the number of returned scores/contexts in edge cases with many invalid chunks; behavior becomes consistent and predictable; should be covered by a regression test that previously triggered the mismatch.

steps.operation: Fix the async per-source-file sampler with the same alignment logic: ensure contexts, scores, and source_files are appended together atomically; avoid appending scores for skipped/invalid contexts and add an invariant check before returning.
steps.reason: Async equivalent of the sync mismatch bug must be fixed to prevent IndexError in async docs/context generation and to keep metadata aligned across all code paths.
steps.side_effects: Async context generation results may change slightly (dropping invalid scored entries); improves correctness; may require adjusting async regression tests.
